items.heading: Scope of a Container
items.source: The entity using a runtime to create a container MUST be able to use the operations defined in this specification against that same container.

items.heading: State
items.source: * **`ociVersion`** (string, REQUIRED) is version of the Open Container Initiative Runtime Specification with which the state complies.

items.heading: State
items.source: * **`id`** (string, REQUIRED) is the container's ID.

items.heading: State
items.source: This MUST be unique across all containers on this host.

items.heading: State
items.source: * **`status`** (string, REQUIRED) is the runtime state of the container.

items.heading: State
items.source: The value MAY be one of:

items.heading: State
items.source: Additional values MAY be defined by the runtime, however, they MUST be used to represent new runtime states not defined above.

items.heading: State
items.source: Additional values MAY be defined by the runtime, however, they MUST be used to represent new runtime states not defined above.

items.heading: State
items.source: * **`pid`** (int, REQUIRED when `status` is `created` or `running` on Linux, OPTIONAL on other platforms) is the ID of the container process.

items.heading: State
items.source: * **`pid`** (int, REQUIRED when `status` is `created` or `running` on Linux, OPTIONAL on other platforms) is the ID of the container process.

items.heading: State
items.source: * **`bundle`** (string, REQUIRED) is the absolute path to the container's bundle directory.

items.heading: State
items.source: * **`annotations`** (map, OPTIONAL) contains the list of annotations associated with the container.

items.heading: State
items.source: If no annotations were provided then this property MAY either be absent or an empty map.

items.heading: State
items.source: The state MAY include additional properties.

items.heading: State
items.source: When serialized in JSON, the format MUST adhere to the JSON Schema [`schema/state-schema.json`](schema/state-schema.json).

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: 2. The container's runtime environment MUST be created according to the configuration in [`config.json`](config.md).

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: If the runtime is unable to create the environment specified in the [`config.json`](config.md), it MUST [generate an error](#errors).

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: While the resources requested in the [`config.json`](config.md) MUST be created, the user-specified program (from [`process`](config.md#process)) MUST NOT be run at this time.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: While the resources requested in the [`config.json`](config.md) MUST be created, the user-specified program (from [`process`](config.md#process)) MUST NOT be run at this time.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: Any updates to [`config.json`](config.md) after this step MUST NOT affect the container.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: 3. The [`prestart` hooks](config.md#prestart) MUST be invoked by the runtime.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: If any `prestart` hook fails, the runtime MUST [generate an error](#errors), stop the container, and continue the lifecycle at step 12.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: 4. The [`createRuntime` hooks](config.md#createRuntime-hooks) MUST be invoked by the runtime.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: If any `createRuntime` hook fails, the runtime MUST [generate an error](#errors), stop the container, and continue the lifecycle at step 12.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: 5. The [`createContainer` hooks](config.md#createContainer-hooks) MUST be invoked by the runtime.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: If any `createContainer` hook fails, the runtime MUST [generate an error](#errors), stop the container, and continue the lifecycle at step 12.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: 7. The [`startContainer` hooks](config.md#startContainer-hooks) MUST be invoked by the runtime.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: If any `startContainer` hook fails, the runtime MUST [generate an error](#errors), stop the container, and continue the lifecycle at step 12.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: 8. The runtime MUST run the user-specified program, as specified by [`process`](config.md#process).

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: 9. The [`poststart` hooks](config.md#poststart) MUST be invoked by the runtime.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: If any `poststart` hook fails, the runtime MUST [generate an error](#errors), stop the container, and continue the lifecycle at step 12.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: This MAY happen due to erroring out, exiting, crashing or the runtime's [`kill`](runtime.md#kill) operation being invoked.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: 12. The container MUST be destroyed by undoing the steps performed during create phase (step 2).

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: 13. The [`poststop` hooks](config.md#poststop) MUST be invoked by the runtime.

items.heading: Lifecycle
items.source: If any `poststop` hook fails, the runtime MUST [log a warning](#warnings), but the remaining hooks and lifecycle continue as if the hook had succeeded.

items.heading: Errors
items.source: Unless otherwise stated, generating an error MUST leave the state of the environment as if the operation were never attempted - modulo any possible trivial ancillary changes such as logging.

items.heading: Warnings
items.source: Unless otherwise stated, logging a warning does not change the flow of the operation; it MUST continue as if the warning had not been logged.

items.heading: Operations
items.source: Unless otherwise stated, runtimes MUST support the following operations.

items.heading: Query State
items.source: This operation MUST [generate an error](#errors) if it is not provided the ID of a container.

items.heading: Query State
items.source: Attempting to query a container that does not exist MUST [generate an error](#errors).

items.heading: Query State
items.source: This operation MUST return the state of a container as specified in the [State](#state) section.

items.heading: Create
items.source: This operation MUST [generate an error](#errors) if it is not provided a path to the bundle and the container ID to associate with the container.

items.heading: Create
items.source: If the ID provided is not unique across all containers within the scope of the runtime, or is not valid in any other way, the implementation MUST [generate an error](#errors) and a new container MUST NOT be created.

items.heading: Create
items.source: If the ID provided is not unique across all containers within the scope of the runtime, or is not valid in any other way, the implementation MUST [generate an error](#errors) and a new container MUST NOT be created.

items.heading: Create
items.source: This operation MUST create a new container.

items.heading: Create
items.source: All of the properties configured in [`config.json`](config.md) except for [`process`](config.md#process) MUST be applied.

items.heading: Create
items.source: [`process.args`](config.md#process) MUST NOT be applied until triggered by the [`start`](#start) operation.

items.heading: Create
items.source: The remaining `process` properties MAY be applied by this operation.

items.heading: Create
items.source: If the runtime cannot apply a property as specified in the [configuration](config.md), it MUST [generate an error](#errors) and a new container MUST NOT be created.

items.heading: Create
items.source: If the runtime cannot apply a property as specified in the [configuration](config.md), it MUST [generate an error](#errors) and a new container MUST NOT be created.

items.heading: Create
items.source: The runtime MAY validate `config.json` against this spec, either generically or with respect to the local system capabilities, before creating the container ([step 2](#lifecycle)).

items.heading: Start
items.source: This operation MUST [generate an error](#errors) if it is not provided the container ID.

items.heading: Start
items.source: Attempting to `start` a container that is not [`created`](#state) MUST have no effect on the container and MUST [generate an error](#errors).

items.heading: Start
items.source: Attempting to `start` a container that is not [`created`](#state) MUST have no effect on the container and MUST [generate an error](#errors).

items.heading: Start
items.source: This operation MUST run the user-specified program as specified by [`process`](config.md#process).

items.heading: Start
items.source: This operation MUST generate an error if `process` was not set.

items.heading: Kill
items.source: This operation MUST [generate an error](#errors) if it is not provided the container ID.

items.heading: Kill
items.source: Attempting to send a signal to a container that is neither [`created` nor `running`](#state) MUST have no effect on the container and MUST [generate an error](#errors).

items.heading: Kill
items.source: Attempting to send a signal to a container that is neither [`created` nor `running`](#state) MUST have no effect on the container and MUST [generate an error](#errors).

items.heading: Kill
items.source: This operation MUST send the specified signal to the container process.

items.heading: Delete
items.source: This operation MUST [generate an error](#errors) if it is not provided the container ID.

items.heading: Delete
items.source: Attempting to `delete` a container that is not [`stopped`](#state) MUST have no effect on the container and MUST [generate an error](#errors).

items.heading: Delete
items.source: Attempting to `delete` a container that is not [`stopped`](#state) MUST have no effect on the container and MUST [generate an error](#errors).

items.heading: Delete
items.source: Deleting a container MUST delete the resources that were created during the `create` step.

items.heading: Delete
items.source: Note that resources associated with the container, but not created by this container, MUST NOT be deleted.

items.heading: Delete
items.source: Once a container is deleted its ID MAY be used by a subsequent container.

items.heading: File descriptors
items.source: The runtime MAY pass additional file descriptors to the application to support features such as [socket activation][socket-activated-containers].

items.heading: File descriptors
items.source: Some of the file descriptors MAY be redirected to `/dev/null` even though they are open.

items.heading: Specification version
items.source: * **`ociVersion`** (string, REQUIRED) MUST be in [SemVer v2.0.0][semver-v2.0.0] format and specifies the version of the Open Container Initiative Runtime Specification with which the bundle complies.

items.heading: Specification version
items.source: * **`ociVersion`** (string, REQUIRED) MUST be in [SemVer v2.0.0][semver-v2.0.0] format and specifies the version of the Open Container Initiative Runtime Specification with which the bundle complies.

items.heading: Root
items.source: **`root`** (object, OPTIONAL) specifies the container's root filesystem.

items.heading: Root
items.source: On Windows, for Windows Server Containers, this field is REQUIRED.

items.heading: Root
items.source: For [Hyper-V Containers](config-windows.md#hyperv), this field MUST NOT be set.

items.heading: Root
items.source: On all other platforms, this field is REQUIRED.

items.heading: Root
items.source: * **`path`** (string, REQUIRED) Specifies the path to the root filesystem for the container.

items.heading: Root
items.source: * On Windows, `path` MUST be a [volume GUID path][naming-a-volume].

items.heading: Root
items.source: The value SHOULD be the conventional `rootfs`.

items.heading: Root
items.source: A directory MUST exist at the path declared by the field.

items.heading: Root
items.source: * **`readonly`** (bool, OPTIONAL) If true then the root filesystem MUST be read-only inside the container, defaults to false.

items.heading: Root
items.source: * **`readonly`** (bool, OPTIONAL) If true then the root filesystem MUST be read-only inside the container, defaults to false.

items.heading: Root
items.source: * On Windows, this field MUST be omitted or false.

items.heading: Mounts
items.source: **`mounts`** (array of objects, OPTIONAL) specifies additional mounts beyond [`root`](#root).

items.heading: Mounts
items.source: The runtime MUST mount entries in the listed order.

items.heading: Mounts
items.source: * **`destination`** (string, REQUIRED) Destination of mount point: path inside container.

items.heading: Mounts
items.source: * Linux: This value SHOULD be an absolute path.

items.heading: Mounts
items.source: For compatibility with old tools and configurations, it MAY be a relative path, in which case it MUST be interpreted as relative to "/".

items.heading: Mounts
items.source: For compatibility with old tools and configurations, it MAY be a relative path, in which case it MUST be interpreted as relative to "/".

items.heading: Mounts
items.source: * Windows: This value MUST be an absolute path.

items.heading: Mounts
items.source: One mount destination MUST NOT be nested within another mount (e.g., c:\\foo and c:\\foo\\bar).

items.heading: Mounts
items.source: * Solaris: This value MUST be an absolute path.

items.heading: Mounts
items.source: * For all other platforms: This value MUST be an absolute path.

items.heading: Mounts
items.source: * **`source`** (string, OPTIONAL) A device name, but can also be a file or directory name for bind mounts or a dummy.

items.heading: Mounts
items.source: * **`options`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) Mount options of the filesystem to be used.

items.heading: Mounts
items.source: * Windows: runtimes MUST support `ro`, mounting the filesystem read-only when `ro` is given.

items.heading: Linux mount options
items.source: `idmap` | SHOULD | Indicates that the mount MUST have an idmapping applied. This option SHOULD NOT be passed to the underlying [`mount(2)`][mount.2] call. If `uidMappings` or `gidMappings` are specified for the mount, the runtime MUST use those values for the mount's mapping. If they are not specified, the runtime MAY use the container's user namespace mapping, otherwise an [error MUST be returned](runtime.md#errors). If there are no `uidMappings` and `gidMappings` specified and the container isn't using user namespaces, an [error MUST be returned](runtime.md#errors). This SHOULD be implemented using [`mount_setattr(MOUNT_ATTR_IDMAP)`][mount_setattr.2], available since Linux 5.12.

If there are no `uidMappings` and `gidMappings` specified and the container isn't using user namespaces, an [error MUST be returned](runtime.md#errors). This SHOULD be implemented using [`mount_setattr(MOUNT_ATTR_IDMAP)`][mount_setattr.2], available since Linux 5.12.

items.source: * **`scheduler`** (object, OPTIONAL) is an object describing the scheduler properties for the process. The `scheduler` contains the following properties:

items.source: * **`nice`** (int32, OPTIONAL) is the nice value for the process, affecting its priority. A lower nice value corresponds to a higher priority. If not set, the runtime must use the value 0.

items.source: * **`priority`** (int32, OPTIONAL) represents the static priority of the process, used by real-time policies like SCHED_FIFO and SCHED_RR. If not set, the runtime must use the value 0.

items.heading: Linux Process

items.heading: Linux Process
items.source: * **`period`** (uint64, OPTIONAL) represents the length of the period in nanoseconds used for determining the process runtime, used by the deadline scheduler. If not set, the runtime must use the value 0.

items.source: The `createContainer` hooks MUST be called as part of the [`create`](runtime.md#create) operation after the runtime environment has been created (according to the configuration in config.json) but before the `pivot_root` or any equivalent operation has been executed.

items.heading: CreateContainer Hooks
items.source: The `createContainer` hooks MUST be called after the `createRuntime` hooks.

items.heading: CreateContainer Hooks
items.source: The `createContainer` hooks' path MUST resolve in the [runtime namespace](glossary.md#runtime-namespace).

items.heading: CreateContainer Hooks
items.source: The `createContainer` hooks MUST be executed in the [container namespace](glossary.md#container-namespace).

items.heading: StartContainer Hooks
items.source: The `startContainer` hooks MUST be called [before the user-specified process is executed](runtime.md#lifecycle) as part of the [`start`](runtime.md#start) operation.

items.heading: StartContainer Hooks
items.source: The `startContainer` hooks' path MUST resolve in the [container namespace](glossary.md#container-namespace).

items.heading: StartContainer Hooks
items.source: The `startContainer` hooks MUST be executed in the [container namespace](glossary.md#container-namespace).

items.heading: Poststart
items.source: The `poststart` hooks MUST be called [after the user-specified process is executed](runtime.md#lifecycle) but before the [`start`](runtime.md#start) operation returns.

items.heading: Poststart
items.source: The `poststart` hooks' path MUST resolve in the [runtime namespace](glossary.md#runtime-namespace).

items.heading: Poststart
items.source: The `poststart` hooks MUST be executed in the [runtime namespace](glossary.md#runtime-namespace).

items.heading: Poststop
items.source: The `poststop` hooks MUST be called [after the container is deleted](runtime.md#lifecycle) but before the [`delete`](runtime.md#delete) operation returns.

items.heading: Poststop
items.source: The `poststop` hooks' path MUST resolve in the [runtime namespace](glossary.md#runtime-namespace).

items.heading: Poststop
items.source: The `poststop` hooks MUST be executed in the [runtime namespace](glossary.md#runtime-namespace).

items.heading: Annotations
items.source: **`annotations`** (object, OPTIONAL) contains arbitrary metadata for the container.

items.heading: Annotations
items.source: This information MAY be structured or unstructured.

items.heading: Annotations
items.source: Annotations MUST be a key-value map.

items.heading: Annotations
items.source: If there are no annotations then this property MAY either be absent or an empty map.

items.heading: Annotations
items.source: Keys MUST be strings.

items.heading: Annotations
items.source: Keys MUST NOT be an empty string.

items.heading: Annotations
items.source: Keys SHOULD be named using a reverse domain notation - e.g. `com.example.myKey`.

items.heading: Annotations
items.source: The `org.opencontainers` namespace for keys is reserved for use by this specification, annotations using keys in this namespace MUST be as described in this section.

items.heading: Annotations

items.heading: Devices
items.source: Devices in FreeBSD are accessed via the `devfs` filesystem. Each container SHOULD have a `devfs` filesystem mounted into its `/dev` directory. Often, a minimal set of devices is exposed to the container using ruleset 4 from `/etc/defaults/devfs.rules` - the ruleset is specified as a mount option.

items.heading: Devices
items.source: * **`path`** _(string, REQUIRED)_ - the device path relative to `/dev`

items.heading: Devices
items.source: * **`mode`** _(uint32, OPTIONAL)_ - file mode for the device.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: * **`parent`** _(string, OPTIONAL)_ - parent jail.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: * **`host`** _(string, OPTIONAL)_ - allow overriding hostname, domainname, hostuuid and hostid.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: * **`ip4`** _(string, OPTIONAL)_ - control the availability of IPv4 addresses.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: * **`ip4Addr`** _(array of strings, OPTIONAL)_ - restrict the set of IPv4 addresses which the container can use. These addresses should be in numeric form (e.g. `"10.11.12.13"`). This can be used to allow restricted use of the host network. A common pattern with FreeBSD jails is to add alias addresses to a loopback interface and restrict each jail to a subset of addresses.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: * **`ip6`** _(string, OPTIONAL)_ - control the availability of IPv6 addresses.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: * **`ip6Addr`** _(array of strings, OPTIONAL)_ - restrict the set of IPv6 addresses which the container can use. These addresses should be in numeric form (e.g. `"fd10::11:12:13"`). This can be used to allow restricted use of the host network. A common pattern with FreeBSD jails is to add alias addresses to a loopback interface and restrict each jail to a subset of addresses.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: * **`vnet`** _(string, OPTIONAL)_ - control the vnet used for this container.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: * **`interface`** _(string, OPTIONAL)_ A network interface to add the container's IP addresses (**`ip4Addr`** and **`ip6Addr`**) to. An alias for each address will be added to the interface when the container is created, and will be removed from the interface after the container is stopped. This is typically used when **`vnet`** is not set.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: * **`vnetInterfaces`** _(array of strings, OPTIONAL)_ - a set of network interfaces which are added to the container's vnet during its lifetime.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: * **`sysvmsg`** _(string, OPTIONAL)_ - allow access to SYSV IPC message primitives.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: * **`sysvsem`** _(string, OPTIONAL)_ - allow access to SYSV IPC semaphore primitives, in the same manner as sysvmsg. Defaults to "new".

items.heading: Jail
items.source: * **`sysvshm`** _(string, OPTIONAL)_ - allow access to SYSV IPC shared memory primitives, in the same manner as sysvmsg. Defaults to "new".

items.heading: Jail
items.source: * **`enforceStatfs`** _(integer, OPTIONAL)_ - control visibility of mounts in the container.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: * **`allow`** _(object, OPTIONAL)_ - Some restrictions of the container environment may be set on a per-container basis. With the exception of **`setHostname`** and **`reservedPorts`**, these boolean parameters are off by default.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: - **`setHostname`** _(bool, OPTIONAL)_ - Allow the container's hostname to be changed. Defaults to `false`.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: - **`rawSockets`** _(bool, OPTIONAL)_ - Allow the container to use raw sockets to support network utilities such as ping and traceroute. Defaults to `false`.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: - **`chflags`** _(bool, OPTIONAL)_ - Allow the system file flags to be changed. Defaults to `false`.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: - **`mount`** _(array of strings, OPTIONAL)_ - Allow the listed filesystem types to be mounted and unmounted in the container.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: - **`quotas`** _(bool, OPTIONAL)_ - Allow the filesystem quotas to be changed in the container. Defaults to `false`.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: - **`socketAf`** _(bool, OPTIONAL)_ - Allow socket types other than IPv4, IPv6 and unix. Defaults to `false`.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: - **`mlock`** _(bool, OPTIONAL)_ - Allow the container to use `mlock(2)` and `munlock(2)` system calls. Defaults to `false`.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: - **`reservedPorts`** _(bool, OPTIONAL)_ - Allow the jail to bind to ports lower than 1024. Defaults to `false`.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: - **`suser`** _(bool, OPTIONAL)_ - The value of the jail's security.bsd.suser_enabled sysctl. The super-user will be disabled automatically if its parent system has it disabled. The super-user is enabled by default.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: These fields SHOULD be mapped to a corresponding set of `jail(8)` parameters which can be used to create the container jail.

items.heading: Jail
items.source: A container which needs its own network namespace SHOULD set `"vnet"` to `"new"` and leave `"ip4"` and `"ip6"` unchanged.

items.heading: Jail

items.heading: PIDs

items.heading: Unified
items.source: **`unified`** (object, OPTIONAL) allows cgroup v2 parameters to be to be set and modified for the container.

items.source: The OCI runtime MUST ensure that the needed cgroup controllers are enabled for the cgroup.

items.heading: Unified

items.source: The runtime MUST generate an error when the configuration refers to a cgroup controller that is not present or that cannot be enabled.

items.heading: Example
items.source: If a controller is enabled on the cgroup v2 hierarchy but the configuration is provided for the cgroup v1 equivalent controller, the runtime MAY attempt a conversion.

items.heading: Example
items.source: If the conversion is not possible the runtime MUST generate an error.

items.source: The following rules on parameters MUST be applied:

items.heading: IntelRdt
items.source: * If both `l3CacheSchema` and `memBwSchema` are set, runtimes MUST write the values to the `schemata` file in that sub-directory discussed in `closID`. The runtimes MUST write `l3CacheSchema` first and `memBwSchema` last.

items.source: * If either `l3CacheSchema` or `memBwSchema` is set, runtimes MUST write the value to the `schemata` file in the that sub-directory discussed in `closID`.

If also `l3CacheSchema` or `memBwSchema` is set the value of `schemata` field must be written last, after the values from `l3CacheSchema` and `memBwSchema` has been written.

If such pre-configured directory `closID` exists, runtime MUST assign container to this `closID` and [generate an error](runtime.md#errors) if directory does not exist.

If such pre-configured directory `closID` exists, runtime MUST assign container to this `closID` and [generate an error](runtime.md#errors) if directory does not exist.

items.heading: Memory policy
items.source: **`memoryPolicy`** (object, OPTIONAL) sets the NUMA memory policy for the container.

items.heading: Memory policy
items.source: * **`mode`** *(string, REQUIRED)* -

items.heading: Memory policy
items.source: * **`nodes`** *(string, OPTIONAL)* - list of memory nodes from which nodemask is constructed to set_mempolicy(2). This is a comma-separated list, with dashes to represent ranges. For example, `0-3,7` represents memory nodes 0,1,2,3, and 7. Some modes require that there are no nodes, e.g. `MPOL_DEFAULT` and `MPOL_LOCAL`. Others that there is at least one node, e.g. `MPOL_BIND` and `MPOL_INTERLEAVE`. See set_mempolicy(2) for details.

items.heading: Seccomp

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: * **`defaultAction`** *(string, REQUIRED)* - the default action for seccomp. Allowed values are the same as `syscalls[].action`.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: * **`defaultErrnoRet`** *(uint, OPTIONAL)* - the errno return code to use.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: When the action doesn't support an errno, the runtime MUST print and error and fail.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: * **`architectures`** *(array of strings, OPTIONAL)* - the architecture used for system calls.

items.source: * **`flags`** *(array of strings, OPTIONAL)* - list of flags to use with seccomp(2).

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: * **`listenerPath`** *(string, OPTIONAL)* - specifies the path of UNIX domain socket over which the runtime will send the [container process state](#containerprocessstate) data structure when the `SCMP_ACT_NOTIFY` action is used.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: This socket MUST use `AF_UNIX` domain and `SOCK_STREAM` type.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: The runtime MUST send exactly one [container process state](#containerprocessstate) per connection.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: The connection MUST NOT be reused and it MUST be closed after sending a seccomp state.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: The connection MUST NOT be reused and it MUST be closed after sending a seccomp state.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: * **`listenerMetadata`** *(string, OPTIONAL)* - specifies an opaque data to pass to the seccomp agent.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: This field MUST NOT be set if `listenerPath` is not set.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: * **`syscalls`** *(array of objects, OPTIONAL)* - match a syscall in seccomp.

items.heading: Seccomp

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: * **`names`** *(array of strings, REQUIRED)* - the names of the syscalls.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: `names` MUST contain at least one entry.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: * **`action`** *(string, REQUIRED)* - the action for seccomp rules.

items.heading: Seccomp

items.heading: Seccomp

items.heading: The Container Process State
items.source: The container runtime MUST send the container process state over the UNIX socket as regular payload serialized in JSON and file descriptors MUST be sent using `SCM_RIGHTS`.

items.heading: The Container Process State
items.source: The container runtime MUST send the container process state over the UNIX socket as regular payload serialized in JSON and file descriptors MUST be sent using `SCM_RIGHTS`.

items.heading: The Container Process State
items.source: The container runtime MAY use several `sendmsg(2)` calls to send the aforementioned data.

items.heading: The Container Process State
items.source: If more than one `sendmsg(2)` is used, the file descriptors MUST be sent only in the first call.

items.heading: The Container Process State
items.source: * **`ociVersion`** (string, REQUIRED) is version of the Open Container Initiative Runtime Specification with which the container process state complies.

items.heading: The Container Process State
items.source: * **`fds`** (array, OPTIONAL) is a string array containing the names of the file descriptors passed.

items.heading: The Container Process State
items.source: * **`pid`** (int, REQUIRED) is the container process ID, as seen by the runtime.

items.heading: The Container Process State
items.source: * **`metadata`** (string, OPTIONAL) opaque metadata.

items.heading: The Container Process State
items.source: * **`state`** ([state](runtime.md#state), REQUIRED) is the state of the container.

items.source: **`maskedPaths`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) will mask over the provided paths inside the container so that they cannot be read.

items.heading: Masked Paths
items.source: The values MUST be absolute paths in the [container namespace](glossary.md#container_namespace).

items.heading: Readonly Paths
items.source: **`readonlyPaths`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) will set the provided paths as readonly inside the container.

items.heading: Readonly Paths
items.source: The values MUST be absolute paths in the [container namespace](glossary.md#container-namespace).

items.heading: Automatic Network (anet)

items.source: * **`lowerLink`** *(string, OPTIONAL)* Specify the link over which the VNIC will be created.

items.heading: Automatic Network (anet)
items.source: * **`allowedAddress`** *(string, OPTIONAL)* The set of IP addresses that the container can use might be constrained by specifying the `allowedAddress` property.

items.heading: LayerFolders
items.source: **`layerFolders`** (array of strings, REQUIRED) specifies a list of layer folders the container image relies on. The list is ordered from topmost layer to base layer with the last entry being the scratch.

items.heading: LayerFolders

items.heading: Devices

items.heading: Resources
items.source: You can configure a container's resource limits via the OPTIONAL `resources` field of the Windows configuration.

items.heading: Memory
items.source: `memory` is an OPTIONAL configuration for the container's memory usage.

items.source: * **`limit`** *(uint64, OPTIONAL)* - sets limit of memory usage in bytes.

items.heading: CPU
items.source: `cpu` is an OPTIONAL configuration for the container's CPU usage.

items.heading: CPU
items.source: * **`count`** *(uint64, OPTIONAL)* - specifies the number of CPUs available to the container. It represents the fraction of the configured processor `count` in a container in relation to the processors available in the host. The fraction ultimately determines the portion of processor cycles that the threads in a container can use during each scheduling interval, as the number of cycles per 10,000 cycles.

items.heading: CPU
items.source: * **`shares`** *(uint16, OPTIONAL)* - limits the share of processor time given to the container relative to other workloads on the processor. The processor `shares` (`weight` at the platform level) is a value between 0 and 10,000.

items.heading: CPU
items.source: * **`maximum`** *(uint16, OPTIONAL)* - determines the portion of processor cycles that the threads in a container can use during each scheduling interval, as the number of cycles per 10,000 cycles. Set processor `maximum` to a percentage times 100.

items.heading: CPU
items.source: * **`affinity`** *(array of objects, OPTIONAL)* - specifies the set of CPU to affinitize for this container.

items.heading: CPU
items.source: * **`mask`** *(uint64, REQUIRED)* - specifies the CPU mask relative to this CPU group.

items.heading: CPU
items.source: * **`group`** *(uint32, REQUIRED)* - specifies the processor group this mask refers to, as returned by GetLogicalProcessorInformationEx.

items.heading: Storage

items.heading: Storage

items.heading: Storage
items.source: * **`bps`** *(uint64, OPTIONAL)* - specifies the maximum bytes per second for the system drive of the container.

items.heading: Storage
items.source: * **`sandboxSize`** *(uint64, OPTIONAL)* - specifies the minimum size of the system drive in bytes.

items.heading: Network
items.source: You can configure a container's networking options via the OPTIONAL `network` field of the Windows configuration.

items.heading: Network
items.source: * **`allowUnqualifiedDNSQuery`** *(bool, OPTIONAL)* - specifies if unqualified DNS name resolution is allowed.

items.heading: Network
items.source: * **`DNSSearchList`** *(array of strings, OPTIONAL)* - comma separated list of DNS suffixes to use for name resolution.

items.heading: Network
items.source: * **`networkSharedContainerName`** *(string, OPTIONAL)* - name (ID) of the container that we will share with the network stack.

items.heading: Network
items.source: * **`networkNamespace`** *(string, OPTIONAL)* - name (ID) of the network namespace that will be used for the container. If a network namespace is specified no other parameter must be specified.

items.heading: Credential Spec
items.source: You can configure a container's group Managed Service Account (gMSA) via the OPTIONAL `credentialSpec` field of the Windows configuration.

items.heading: Servicing
items.source: You can indicate that a container should be started in a mode to apply pending servicing operations via the OPTIONAL `servicing` field of the Windows configuration.

items.heading: IgnoreFlushesDuringBoot
items.source: You can indicate that a container should be started in a mode where disk flushes are not performed during container boot via the OPTIONAL `ignoreFlushesDuringBoot` field of the Windows configuration.

items.heading: HyperV
items.source: `hyperv` is an OPTIONAL field of the Windows configuration.

items.heading: HyperV
items.source: If present, the container MUST be run with Hyper-V isolation.

items.heading: HyperV
items.source: If omitted, the container MUST be run as a Windows Server container.

items.heading: HyperV
items.source: * **`utilityVMPath`** *(string, OPTIONAL)* - specifies the path to the image used for the utility VM.

items.heading: Hypervisor Object
items.source: This value MUST be an absolute path in the [runtime mount namespace](glossary.md#runtime-namespace).

items.heading: Hypervisor Object
items.source: * **`parameters`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) specifies an array of parameters to pass to the hypervisor.

items.heading: Kernel Object
items.source: **`kernel`** (object, REQUIRED) specifies details of the kernel to boot the container virtual machine with.

items.heading: Kernel Object
items.source: * **`path`** (string, REQUIRED) path to the kernel used to boot the container virtual machine.

items.heading: Kernel Object
items.source: This value MUST be an absolute path in the [runtime mount namespace](glossary.md#runtime-namespace).

items.heading: Kernel Object
items.source: * **`parameters`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) specifies an array of parameters to pass to the kernel.

items.heading: Kernel Object
items.source: * **`initrd`** (string, OPTIONAL) path to an initial ramdisk to be used by the container virtual machine.

items.heading: Kernel Object
items.source: This value MUST be an absolute path in the [runtime mount namespace](glossary.md#runtime-namespace).

items.heading: Image Object
items.source: **`image`** (object, OPTIONAL) specifies details of the image that contains the root filesystem for the container virtual machine.

items.heading: Image Object
items.source: * **`path`** (string, REQUIRED) path to the container virtual machine root image.

items.heading: Image Object
items.source: This value MUST be an absolute path in the [runtime mount namespace](glossary.md#runtime-namespace).

items.heading: Image Object
items.source: * **`format`** (string, REQUIRED) format of the container virtual machine root image. Commonly supported formats are:

items.heading: HWConfig Object
items.source: **`hwConfig`** (object OPTIONAL) Specifies the hardware configuration that should be passed to the VM.

items.heading: HWConfig Object
items.source: * **`deviceTree`** (string OPTIONAL) Path to the container device-tree file that should be passed to the VM.

items.heading: HWConfig Object
items.source: * **`vcpus`** (int OPTIONAL) Number of virtual cpus for the VM.

items.heading: HWConfig Object
items.source: * **`memory`** (int OPTIONAL) Maximum memory in bytes allocated to the VM.

items.heading: HWConfig Object
items.source: * **`dtdevs`** (array OPTIONAL) Host device tree nodes to passthrough to the VM, see [Xen Config][xl-config-format] for the details.

items.heading: HWConfig Object
items.source: * **`iomems`** (array OPTIONAL) Allow auto-translated domains to access specific hardware I/O memory pages, see [Xen Config][xl-config-format].

items.heading: HWConfig Object
items.source: * **`firstGFN`** (int OPTIONAL) Guest Frame Number to map the iomem range.

items.heading: HWConfig Object
items.source: * **`firstMFN`** (int REQUIRED) Physical page number of iomem regions, see [Xen Config][xl-config-format] for the details.

items.heading: HWConfig Object
items.source: * **`nrMFNs`** (int REQUIRED) Number of pages to be mapped, see [Xen Config][xl-config-format] for the details.

items.heading: HWConfig Object
items.source: * **`irqs`** (array OPTIONAL) Allows VM to access specific physical IRQs, see [Xen Config][xl-config-format] for the details.

items.heading: z/OS Container Configuration
items.source: The following filesystems SHOULD be made available in each container's filesystem:

items.heading: Namespaces
items.source: * **`type`** *(string, REQUIRED)* - namespace type. The following namespace types SHOULD be supported:

items.heading: Namespaces
items.source: * **`type`** *(string, REQUIRED)* - namespace type. The following namespace types SHOULD be supported:

items.heading: Namespaces
items.source: * **`path`** *(string, OPTIONAL)* - namespace file.

items.heading: Namespaces
items.source: This value MUST be an absolute path in the [runtime mount namespace](glossary.md#runtime-namespace).

items.heading: Namespaces
items.source: The runtime MUST place the container process in the namespace associated with that `path`.

items.heading: Namespaces
items.source: The runtime MUST [generate an error](runtime.md#errors) if `path` is not associated with a namespace of type `type`.

items.heading: Namespaces
items.source: If `path` is not specified, the runtime MUST create a new [container namespace](glossary.md#container-namespace) of type `type`.

items.heading: Namespaces
items.source: If a namespace type is not specified in the `namespaces` array, the container MUST inherit the [runtime namespace](glossary.md#runtime-namespace) of that type.

items.heading: Namespaces
items.source: If a `namespaces` field contains duplicated namespaces with same `type`, the runtime MUST [generate an error](runtime.md#errors).

items.heading: Features Structure
items.source: A [runtime](glossary.md#runtime) MAY provide a JSON structure about its implemented features to [runtime callers](glossary.md#runtime-caller).

items.heading: Features Structure
items.source: Hence, the content of the Features structure SHOULD be determined on the compilation time of the runtime, not on the execution time.

items.heading: Features Structure
items.source: All properties in the Features structure except `ociVersionMin` and `ociVersionMax` MAY either be absent or have the `null` value.

items.heading: Features Structure
items.source: The `null` value MUST NOT be confused with an empty value such as `0`, `false`, `""`, `[]`, and `{}`.

items.heading: Specification version
items.source: * **`ociVersionMin`** (string, REQUIRED) The minimum recognized version of the Open Container Initiative Runtime Specification.

items.heading: Specification version
items.source: The runtime MUST accept this value as the [`ociVersion` property of `config.json`](config.md#specification-version).

items.heading: Specification version
items.source: * **`ociVersionMax`** (string, REQUIRED) The maximum recognized version of the Open Container Initiative Runtime Specification.

items.heading: Specification version
items.source: The runtime MUST accept this value as the [`ociVersion` property of `config.json`](config.md#specification-version).

items.heading: Specification version
items.source: The value MUST NOT be less than the value of the `ociVersionMin` property.

items.heading: Specification version
items.source: The Features structure MUST NOT contain properties that are not defined in this version of the Open Container Initiative Runtime Specification.

items.heading: Hooks
items.source: * **`hooks`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) The recognized names of the [hooks](config.md#posix-platform-hooks).

items.heading: Hooks
items.source: The runtime MUST support the elements in this array as the [`hooks` property of `config.json`](config.md#posix-platform-hooks).

items.heading: Mount Options
items.source: * **`mountOptions`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) The recognized names of the mount options, including options that might not be supported by the host operating system.

items.heading: Mount Options
items.source: The runtime MUST recognize the elements in this array as the [`options` of `mounts` objects in `config.json`](config.md#mounts).

items.heading: Mount Options
items.source: * Linux: this array SHOULD NOT contain filesystem-specific mount options that are passed to the [mount(2)][mount.2] syscall as `const void *data`.

items.heading: Platform-specific features
items.source: * **`linux`** (object, OPTIONAL) [Linux-specific features](features-linux.md).

items.heading: Platform-specific features
items.source: This MAY be set if the runtime supports `linux` platform.

items.heading: Annotations
items.source: **`annotations`** (object, OPTIONAL) contains arbitrary metadata of the runtime.

items.heading: Annotations
items.source: This information MAY be structured or unstructured.

items.heading: Annotations
items.source: Annotations MUST be a key-value map that follows the same convention as the Key and Values of the [`annotations` property of `config.json`](config.md#annotations).

items.heading: Unsafe annotations in `config.json`
items.source: **`potentiallyUnsafeConfigAnnotations`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) contains values of [`annotations` property of `config.json`](config.md#annotations)

items.heading: Namespaces
items.source: * **`namespaces`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) The recognized names of the namespaces, including namespaces that might not be supported by the host operating system.

items.heading: Namespaces
items.source: The runtime MUST recognize the elements in this array as the [`type` of `linux.namespaces` objects in `config.json`](config-linux.md#namespaces).

items.heading: Capabilities
items.source: * **`capabilities`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) The recognized names of the capabilities, including capabilities that might not be supported by the host operating system.

items.heading: Capabilities
items.source: The runtime MUST recognize the elements in this array in the [`process.capabilities` object of `config.json`](config.md#linux-process).

items.heading: Cgroup
items.source: **`cgroup`** (object, OPTIONAL) represents the runtime's implementation status of cgroup managers.

items.heading: Cgroup
items.source: * **`v1`** (bool, OPTIONAL) represents whether the runtime supports cgroup v1.

items.heading: Cgroup
items.source: * **`v2`** (bool, OPTIONAL) represents whether the runtime supports cgroup v2.

items.heading: Cgroup
items.source: * **`systemd`** (bool, OPTIONAL) represents whether the runtime supports system-wide systemd cgroup manager.

items.heading: Cgroup
items.source: * **`systemdUser`** (bool, OPTIONAL) represents whether the runtime supports user-scoped systemd cgroup manager.

items.heading: Cgroup
items.source: * **`rdma`** (bool, OPTIONAL) represents whether the runtime supports RDMA cgroup controller.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: **`seccomp`** (object, OPTIONAL) represents the runtime's implementation status of seccomp.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: * **`enabled`** (bool, OPTIONAL) represents whether the runtime supports seccomp.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: * **`actions`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) The recognized names of the seccomp actions.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: The runtime MUST recognize the elements in this array in the [`syscalls[].action` property of the `linux.seccomp` object in `config.json`](config-linux.md#seccomp).

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: * **`operators`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) The recognized names of the seccomp operators.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: The runtime MUST recognize the elements in this array in the [`syscalls[].args[].op` property of the `linux.seccomp` object in `config.json`](config-linux.md#seccomp).

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: * **`archs`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) The recognized names of the seccomp architectures.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: The runtime MUST recognize the elements in this array in the [`architectures` property of the `linux.seccomp` object in `config.json`](config-linux.md#seccomp).

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: * **`knownFlags`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) The recognized names of the seccomp flags.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: The runtime MUST recognize the elements in this array in the [`flags` property of the `linux.seccomp` object in `config.json`](config-linux.md#seccomp).

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: * **`supportedFlags`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL) The recognized and supported names of the seccomp flags.

items.heading: Seccomp
items.source: The runtime MUST recognize and support the elements in this array in the [`flags` property of the `linux.seccomp` object in `config.json`](config-linux.md#seccomp).

items.heading: AppArmor
items.source: **`apparmor`** (object, OPTIONAL) represents the runtime's implementation status of AppArmor.

items.heading: AppArmor
items.source: * **`enabled`** (bool, OPTIONAL) represents whether the runtime supports AppArmor.

items.heading: SELinux
items.source: **`selinux`** (object, OPTIONAL) represents the runtime's implementation status of SELinux.

items.heading: SELinux
items.source: * **`enabled`** (bool, OPTIONAL) represents whether the runtime supports SELinux.

items.heading: MemoryPolicy
items.source: **`memoryPolicy`** (object, OPTIONAL) represents the runtime's implementation status of memoryPolicy.

items.heading: MemoryPolicy
items.source: * **`modes`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL). Recognized memory policies. Includes policies that may not be supported by the host operating system.

items.heading: MemoryPolicy
items.source: The runtime MUST recognize the elements in this array as the [`mode` of `linux.memoryPolicy` objects in `config.json`](config-linux.md#memory-policy).

items.heading: MemoryPolicy
items.source: * **`flags`** (array of strings, OPTIONAL). Recognized flags for memory policies. Includes flags that may not be supported by the host operating system.

items.heading: MemoryPolicy
items.source: The runtime MUST recognize the elements in this in the [`flags` property of the `linux.memoryPolicy` object in `config.json`](config-linux.md#memory-policy)

items.heading: Intel RDT
items.source: **`intelRdt`** (object, OPTIONAL) represents the runtime's implementation status of Intel RDT.

items.heading: Intel RDT
items.source: * **`enabled`** (bool, OPTIONAL) represents whether the runtime supports Intel RDT.

items.heading: MountExtensions
items.source: **`mountExtensions`** (object, OPTIONAL) represents whether the runtime supports certain mount features, irrespective of the availability of the features on the host operating system.

items.heading: MountExtensions
items.source: * **`idmap`** (object, OPTIONAL) represents whether the runtime supports idmap mounts using the `uidMappings` and `gidMappings` properties of the mount.

items.heading: MountExtensions
items.source: * **`enabled`** (bool, OPTIONAL) represents whether the runtime parses and attempts to use the `uidMappings` and `gidMappings` properties of mounts if provided.

items.heading: MountExtensions
items.source: In such cases, runtimes MUST still set this value to `true`, to indicate that the runtime recognises the `uidMappings` and `gidMappings` properties.

items.heading: NetDevices
items.source: **`netDevices`** (object, OPTIONAL) represents the runtime's implementation status of Linux network devices.

items.heading: NetDevices
items.source: * **`enabled`** (bool, OPTIONAL) represents whether the runtime supports the capability to move Linux network devices into the container's network namespace.

items.heading: JSON
items.source: All configuration [JSON][] MUST be encoded in [UTF-8][].

items.heading: JSON
items.source: JSON objects MUST NOT include duplicate names.
